Location and tag filters use the 'OR' operation. If more than one is selected only one will be
 required for a match.
items.tip_2: Searches prefixed with '#'' will query for a asset ID (example '#000-001')
items.tip_3:
Field filters use the 'OR' operation. If more than one is selected only one will be required for a
 match.

label_generator.edit_layout: Edit label layout in site settings
label_generator.instruction_1:
The {appName} Label Generator is a tool to help you print labels for your {appName} inventory. These are intended to
 be print-ahead labels so you can print many labels and have them ready to apply
label_generator.instruction_2:
As such, these labels work by printing a URL QR Code and AssetID information on a label. If you've disabled
 AssetID's in your {appName} settings, you can still use this tool, but the AssetID's won't reference any item
label_generator.tip_1: The defaults here are setup for the ''Avery 5260 label sheets''. If you're using a different sheet, you'll need to adjust the settings to match your sheet.
label_generator.tip_2: If you're customizing your sheet the dimensions are in inches. When building the 5260 sheet, I found that the dimensions used in their template, did not match what was needed to print within the boxes. ''Be prepared for some trial and error.''
label_generator.tip_3:
When printing be sure to: '
  1. 'Set the margins to 0 or None'
  2. 'Set the scaling to 100%'
  3. 'Disable double-sided printing'
  4. 'Print a test page before printing multiple pages'
'
label_generator.title: Label Generator

setup.name: Name
setup.subtitle: Create your administrator account to get started.
setup.title: Welcome to {appName}

tags.tag_delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this tag? This action cannot be undone.

actions_set.ensure_ids_confirm: Are you sure you want to ensure all assets have an ID? This can take a while and cannot be undone.
actions_set.ensure_ids_sub: Ensures that all items in your inventory have a valid asset_id field. This is done by finding the highest current asset_id field in the database and applying the next value to each item that has an unset asset_id field. This is done in order of the created_at field.
actions_set.ensure_import_refs_sub: Ensures that all items in your inventory have a valid import_ref field. This is done by randomly generating a 8 character string for each item that has an unset import_ref field.

tools.actions_sub: Apply Actions to your inventory in bulk. These are irreversible actions. ''Be careful.''

backups_set.create_sub: Builds a zip with every entity, tag, custom field, attachment, and maintenance record in this collection. The job runs in the background; the artifact will appear in the table below when it's ready.
backups_set.delete_confirm: Delete this backup artifact? This cannot be undone.
backups_set.failed: Backup failed. Check server logs for details.
backups_set.restore_sub: Upload a backup zip to populate this collection. The collection must contain no items yet — default locations and tags are wiped automatically as part of the restore.

tools.backups_sub: Export your entire collection — entities, tags, attachments, and metadata — to a portable zip archive. Restore an archive into a freshly registered, empty collection on this or another server.

import_export_set.export_sub: Exports the standard CSV format for {appName}. This will export all items in your inventory.
import_export_set.import_ref_confirm: Are you sure you want to ensure all assets have an import_ref? This can take a while and cannot be undone.
import_export_set.import_sub: Imports the standard CSV format for {appName}. Without an ''HB.import_ref'' column, this will ''not'' overwrite any existing items in your inventory, only add new items. Rows with an ''HB.import_ref'' column are merged into existing items with the same import_ref, if one exists.

tools.import_export_sub: Import and export your inventory to and from a CSV file. This is useful for migrating your inventory to a new instance of {appName}.

reports_set.asset_labels_sub: Generates a printable PDF of labels for a range of Asset ID. These are not specific to your inventory so you are able to print labels ahead of time and apply them to your inventory when you receive them.
reports_set.bill_of_materials_sub: Generates a CSV (Comma Separated Values) file that can be imported into a spreadsheet program. This is a summary of your inventory with basic item and pricing information.

tools.reports_sub: Generate different reports for your inventory.

toast.backup_started: Backup started — it will appear in the table when ready.
toast.restore_requires_empty: Restore requires a collection with no items. Switch to a freshly registered collection (default locations and tags are fine) and try again.
toast.restore_started: Restore started — refresh shortly to see imported items.
